Leukocyte esterase [Presence] in Urine by Test strip

tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: Renal function is impaired (elevated creatinine, proteinuria), with low urine specific gravity suggesting possible chronic kidney disease. Urinalysis shows protein, glucose, ketones, and abnormal appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), but no evidence of infection (negative nitrite/leukocytes). Blood glucose and urea are normal; HbA1c is low, possibly due to anemia or recent blood loss. Recommend further evaluation of renal function and possible underlying causes.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
